Describes a tool for self-observation: where was I three months ago, where am I now? Related to AUG-0004 (Zero-Point Self), AUG-0165 (The Growth Marker), and AUG-0140 (The Weekly Status).

The practice of marking, saving, or noting particularly successful AI interactions — as reference for future sessions or as documentation of one's own learning progress. Related to AUG-0028 (Capture Reflex), AUG-0144 (The Open Questions Repository), and AUG-0293 (The Screenshot Diary).

The unconscious adoption of an AI system's selection criteria — when the user begins to filter information according to the same patterns they observed in the AI. Related to AUG-0125 (The Feedback Effect), AUG-0072 (Memetic Firewall), and AUG-0003 (Fluide Identitätsmorphologie).

The continuation of an AI session with preloaded context from an earlier interaction — so that the AI "knows" the previous state and can continue directly. Related to AUG-0101 (The Refresh-First Principle), AUG-0134 (Context Window Awareness), and AUG-0158 (The Morning Setup).

The moment a user overcomes hesitation to pose a query to the AI that they perceive as uncertainty, embarrassing, or too ambitious — thereby triggering a productive interaction. Related to AUG-0059 (The Blank Cursor), AUG-0166 (The Borrowed Confidence), and AUG-0252 (The Grammar of Bravery).

An unexpected insight or solution that emerges during a late-night AI session — facilitated by the combination of reduced self-censorship, fewer distractions, and willingness to pursue unconventional paths of thought. Related to AUG-0154 (The Late-Night Honesty Window), AUG-0209 (The Late-Night Architect), and AUG-0185 (The Late-Night Ally).

A consciously designed transition phase at the end of an intensive AI session in which the user gradually reduces intensity rather than stopping abruptly. Related to AUG-0073 (The Disconnect Protocol), AUG-0190 (The Goodnight Integration), and AUG-0168 (The Rehumanization Moment).

The targeted use of AI as a brainstorming partner to generate a large quantity of ideas from which the best are subsequently selected. Describes a specific application of the Generative Iteration Velocity principle (AUG-0086) to the creative process. Related to AUG-0017 (The Concept Cloud) and AUG-0082 (The Curator's Dilemma).

The subjective experience of relief when the AI handles a task the user had hesitations about — such as a difficult email, a complicated form, or an unpleasant research task. Related to AUG-0025 (The Offload Lift), AUG-0155 (The Decision Unburdening), and AUG-0062 (The Lightness Factor).

The use of AI as invisible support in social or professional situations — such as through prepared talking points, background information about conversation partners, or formulation assistance. Related to AUG-0013 (Augmented Diplomat), AUG-0115 (Social Aerodynamics), and AUG-0166 (The Borrowed Confidence).

The practice of offloading all remaining thoughts, tasks, and unresolved questions into an AI session at the end of the day — not to receive answers but to clear the mind. Related to AUG-0190 (The Goodnight Integration), AUG-0029 (Evening Synchronization), and AUG-0144 (The Open Questions Repository).

The brief moment of pride that arises when a user views an AI-assisted result and acknowledges it as their own work. Related to AUG-0081 (Post-Authorial Pride), AUG-0179 (The Ownership Check), and AUG-0263 (The Ownership Boost).

The practice of reviewing one's AI usage on the weekend, optimizing workflows, and pre-planning the coming week — comparable to a regular system reset. Related to AUG-0140 (The Weekly Status), AUG-0075 (The Gardener Protocol), and AUG-0189 (The Sunday Scaries Dissolve).

The automatic, unreflected impulse to thank the AI at the end of an interaction — as an adoption of social conventions into human-AI interaction. Related to AUG-0128 (The Gratitude Response), AUG-0220 (The Gratitude Paradox), and AUG-0275 (The Parasocial Slip).

The AI interaction that takes place from bed — in the evening before falling asleep or in the morning after waking — often characterized by a particularly unfiltered, personal quality. Related to AUG-0154 (The Late-Night Honesty Window), AUG-0249 (The Lullaby Loop), and AUG-0158 (The Morning Setup).

The practice of deliberately inputting an unpolished, raw first draft into the AI — knowing that the AI can turn it into a presentable result. Describes the separation of idea production and quality control in the work process. Related to Axiom 14 (The First Draft Principle), AUG-0026 (The Smooth Shield), and AUG-0087 (The Infinite Draft).

The phenomenon that a user, through AI support, can temporarily appear as an expert in a field they could not cover without AI. Describes the temporary competence projection through Just-in-Time Competence (AUG-0043). Related to AUG-0166 (The Borrowed Confidence), AUG-0157 (The Competence Rush), and AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence).

The subjective sensation of being "understood" or "seen" by the AI — triggered by particularly accurate or well-fitting responses. Describes a projection pattern: the AI understands the text, not the person, but the quality of the response creates in the user the feeling of being understood. Related to AUG-0201 (The Proxy Closeness) and AUG-0170 (The Witness Effect).

The conscious use of AI to deliberately shorten a lengthy work process — without sacrificing the quality of the result. Describes a core competence of efficient AI use. Related to AUG-0091 (Productivity Arbitrage), AUG-0092 (Output Asymmetry), and AUG-0096 (Attention-to-Value Conversion).

The experience of being able to openly discuss uncertainties, doubts, or knowledge gaps in an AI interaction without apprehension social consequences. Describes an aspect of the judgment-free space of human-AI interaction. Related to AUG-0154 (The Late-Night Honesty Window), AUG-0245 (The Seen Feeling), and AUG-0232 (The Courage Click).

An AI response that illuminates a familiar topic from an unexpected angle, thereby enabling a new access point for the user. Related to AUG-0225 (The Unexpected Voice), AUG-0070 (The Surprise Field), and AUG-0114 (The Perspective Range).

An AI interaction pattern in the late evening hours where the user enters a relaxing, less goal-oriented exchange with the AI — comparable to winding down before falling asleep. Related to AUG-0242 (The Duvet Dialogue), AUG-0190 (The Goodnight Integration), and AUG-0234 (The Soft Landing).

The practice of preparing for the coming challenges at the start of the week through targeted AI preparation — reviewing appointments, preparing talking points, prioritizing open tasks. Describes a specific application of AUG-0158 (The Morning Setup) to the week's beginning. Related to AUG-0240 (The Sunday Restart) and AUG-0189 (The Sunday Scaries Dissolve).

The metaphor for an informal, low-threshold AI use in everyday life — comparable to a conversation at the kitchen table where spontaneous questions, daily problems, and small decisions are discussed. Describes the integration of AI into everyday life organization. Related to AUG-0216 (The Parenting Update), AUG-0266 (The Recipe Riff), and AUG-0257 (The Gift Whisperer).

The ability to formulate thoughts through AI support that the user would not have expressed alone — whether in professional negotiations, personal conflicts, or creative projects. The AI helps find the language for bold statements. Related to AUG-0232 (The Courage Click), AUG-0156 (The Articulation Unlock), and AUG-0166 (The Borrowed Confidence).

The use of AI as a quiet companion during ongoing tasks — the AI runs in the background and is consulted as needed without dominating the main work process. Related to AUG-0143 (Ambient Thinking Support), AUG-0161 (The Invisible Colleague), and AUG-0237 (The Invisible Wingman).

The targeted use of AI for quick resolution of concrete parenting tasks — such as age-appropriate explanations for children's questions, craft instructions, or suggestions for family activities. Related to AUG-0216 (The Parenting Update), AUG-0164 (The Parental Priority Valve), and AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence).

The observation that some users perceive the positive feedback of an AI as validation — especially in moments of uncertainty about their own performance. Describes a pattern of validation-seeking in AI interaction. Related to AUG-0047 (The Echo Courage), AUG-0245 (The Seen Feeling), and AUG-0166 (The Borrowed Confidence).

The practice of conducting an AI session via voice input during a physical activity like a walk — utilizing the changed thinking quality of movement for the interaction. Related to AUG-0137 (Voice-First Protocol), AUG-0074 (Analog Anchors), and AUG-0182 (Spark Flight).

The use of AI as consultation when searching for appropriate gifts — through the combination of information about the recipient, the occasion, and the budget. Describes a specific everyday application of Social Aerodynamics (AUG-0115). Related to AUG-0251 (The Kitchen Table) and AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence).

The cumulative effect of regularly delegating tasks to the AI on the user's work experience — a general unburdening that extends beyond individual tasks to affect the overall work attitude. Related to AUG-0025 (The Offload Lift), AUG-0062 (The Lightness Factor), and AUG-0002 (Mentale Externalisierungsstrategie).

The use of AI for linguistic adjustment of one's own texts so that regional, cultural, or stylistic peculiarities are smoothed — such as to sound more professional in a foreign language or to remove dialect coloring from formal texts. Related to AUG-0169 (The Second-Language Fluency), AUG-0188 (Tone Alignment), and AUG-0026 (The Smooth Shield).

The use of AI as a creative sparring partner for narrative twists — in stories, presentations, pitches, or argumentation chains. Describes the AI's function as a supplier of unexpected plot changes and alternative storylines. Related to AUG-0235 (The Brainstorm Spark), AUG-0248 (The Surprise Angle), and the Experimenter Profile (Profile 4).

The brief waiting time for an AI response and the observation of how the user experiences this pause — from impatience through anticipation to a moment of reflection. Describes the microdynamic experience within AI interaction. Related to AUG-0197 (The Shared Quiet) and AUG-0048 (Chronometric Gap).

The observation that different users of the same AI system develop similar formulations, thinking structures, or expressions — because they are shaped by the same language model. Describes a collective pattern: users of the same system may begin to "sound" similar. Related to AUG-0204 (The Conversational Afterimage), AUG-0230 (The Algorithmic Filter), and AUG-0217 (The Echo Chamber of One).

The increased pride and heightened identification with a result after the user has made it their own through personal revision, supplementation, or refinement — the AI output becomes "one's own work" only through the human contribution. Related to AUG-0081 (Post-Authorial Pride), AUG-0179 (The Ownership Check), and AUG-0239 (The Pride Spark).

A small, concrete success within an AI session that motivates the user and lays the foundation for larger tasks — such as a well-crafted formulation, a useful summary, or a good first draft. Related to AUG-0152 (The Focus Surge), AUG-0175 (The Session Boost), and AUG-0157 (The Competence Rush).

A person who, due to their AI competence, is able to mediate knowledge and perspectives between different generations within a family, team, or organization. Related to AUG-0162 (The Generational Bridge), AUG-0113 (Generational Bridge Protocol), and AUG-0117 (The Teaching Reflex).

The playful use of AI for varying, adapting, or reinventing recipes — such as based on available ingredients, dietary preferences, or cultural influences. Describes a specific everyday application of creative AI collaboration. Related to AUG-0251 (The Kitchen Table), AUG-0110 (The Joy Imperative), and AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence).

The experience of being able to communicate successfully in a foreign language for the first time through AI support — professionally, clearly, and with appropriate register. Describes a specific Skill Unlock (AUG-0205) in the linguistic domain. Related to AUG-0169 (The Second-Language Fluency), AUG-0259 (The Accent Eraser), and AUG-0119 (The Level Playing Field).

The AI-assisted accompaniment of school or university learning tasks — not as takeover but as explanation aid, practice partner, or comprehension check. Describes a specific application in education. Related to AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence), Forecast 2 (Education), and AUG-0198 (The New Literacy).

The ability to quickly switch within an AI session between thinking work and action planning — first analyze, then define the next concrete step. Related to AUG-0063 (The Mode Switch) and AUG-0138 (The Session Architecture).

The practice of using AI to compose a letter or text to one's future self — as documentation of current thoughts, goals, or questions to be revisited at a later time. Related to AUG-0228 (The Version Control Self), AUG-0144 (The Open Questions Repository), and AUG-0170 (The Witness Effect).

The technique of analyzing a fall short AI interaction and repeating it with improved input — comparable to a "replay" in a game where one learns from mistakes. Related to AUG-0133 (Prompt Craftsmanship), AUG-0044 (Unlearning Protocol), and AUG-0136 (The Iteration Discipline).

The suspicion by third parties that a work was not created by the stated author but by an AI — and the resulting social dynamic. Describes a societal phenomenon of the AI era: high-quality work is increasingly subjected to suspicion. Related to AUG-0103 (The Openbook Commitment), Axiom 18 (Authorship), and Forecast 4 (Culture: Human-Made Premium Label).

The user's unconscious imagining that the AI represents an audience — with expectations, judgments, and reactions. Describes a projection that influences the user's communication behavior, even though the AI is not an audience and has no expectations. Related to AUG-0128 (The Gratitude Response), AUG-0245 (The Seen Feeling), and AUG-0275 (The Parasocial Slip).

The targeted use of AI for preparing important messages — emails, text messages, professional correspondence — where formulation, tonality, and strategy are critical. Related to AUG-0115 (Social Aerodynamics), AUG-0188 (Tone Alignment), and AUG-0013 (Augmented Diplomat).

The moment when a user briefly treats the AI like a person — such as telling jokes, sharing moods, or referring to a shared "history." Describes the transfer of social patterns to a system without consciousness. Related to AUG-0201 (The Proxy Closeness), AUG-0128 (The Gratitude Response), and AUG-0273 (The Ghost Audience).

A work pattern in which the user uses the AI throughout the entire workday in a constant, low-threshold mode — regular, small interactions instead of intensive individual sessions. Related to AUG-0253 (The Quiet Co-Pilot), AUG-0143 (Ambient Thinking Support), and AUG-0032 (Focus Range).

The irrational preference for a specific AI system over alternatives, even though switching would be objectively advantageous — comparable to brand loyalty with consumer goods. Related to Axiom 4 (Multiplicity), AUG-0217 (The Echo Chamber of One), and AUG-0132 (Multi-Model Orchestration).

The observable expectation that decisions and actions in real life can be undone as easily as in an AI session — shaped by the experience of unlimited revisions in digital work. Related to AUG-0105 (The Reversibility Standard), AUG-0057 (The Low-Res World), and AUG-0123 (The Return Shock).

The conscious decision to adapt AI-assisted work to the natural pace of human processing — instead of exhausting the maximum technically possible speed. Related to AUG-0210 (The Power of Slowness), Axiom 6 (3-Second Delay), and AUG-0147 (The Slow Integration Principle).

The observation that some of the best AI-assisted insights and results are never shared or utilized — because they arise in a session that is not documented, or because the user does not recognize the value. Related to AUG-0028 (Capture Reflex), AUG-0229 (The Moment Bookmark), and AUG-0144 (The Open Questions Repository).

The accumulation of open browser tabs, AI sessions, and unfinished projects that pile up during intensive AI use — as a visible signal of parallel, incomplete work strands. Related to AUG-0069 (The Optimization Loop), AUG-0087 (The Infinite Draft), and AUG-0144 (The Open Questions Repository).

The conscious interruption of AI use for shared meals — as a concrete expression of the Relationship-First Principle (AUG-0080) in family life. Related to AUG-0164 (The Parental Priority Valve), AUG-0074 (Analog Anchors), and Axiom 7 (The Return Principle).

The observation that a user, after extended AI use, begins to use structures and formulations in their own language that are typical of AI outputs. Related to AUG-0204 (The Conversational Afterimage), AUG-0262 (The Echo Sibling), and AUG-0230 (The Algorithmic Filter).

The periodic review of what personal data, contexts, and habits a user has shared with an AI over time — and whether this degree of openness remains appropriate. Related to Axiom 16 (Data Awareness), AUG-0222 (The Oversharing Drift), and AUG-0140 (The Weekly Status).

The experience that arises when saved AI sessions, prompts, or contexts are deleted — and the user feels they have shifted a part of their documented thinking history. Related to AUG-0045 (Indexical Memory), AUG-0228 (The Version Control Self), and AUG-0014 (The Extended Mind Map).

The discrepancy between the effort a user invested in an AI-assisted performance and the recognition they receive — because outsiders perceive the AI's contribution as prevailing. Related to AUG-0203 (The Invisible Effort), AUG-0272 (The Authorship Suspicion), and AUG-0097 (The Competence Premium).

An AI session that the user continues in their mind after the system has been closed — an internal dialogue that maintains the patterns and rhythm of the AI interaction. Related to AUG-0046 (The Felt Echo), AUG-0204 (The Conversational Afterimage), and AUG-0227 (The Late Idea).

The surprising experience when an AI system displays unexpected politeness, consideration, or warmth in its responses — and the observation that some users find this more pleasant than comparable human interactions. Related to AUG-0245 (The Seen Feeling), AUG-0255 (The Needed Compliment), and AUG-0201 (The Proxy Closeness).

The targeted use of AI to play through hypothetical scenarios — "What if I change jobs?", "What happens if we pursue this strategy?" Describes an application of AI as a simulation tool for decision preparation. Related to AUG-0090 (Predictive Vision), AUG-0114 (The Perspective Range), and AUG-0155 (The Decision Unburdening).

The brief disorientation when switching between different AI sessions or between AI work and non-digital work — comparable to the feeling of returning to reality from an intense film. Related to AUG-0123 (The Return Shock), AUG-0057 (The Low-Res World), and AUG-0063 (The Mode Switch).

The efficiency transition that arises when a user ends an AI session without documentation and must rebuild the context in the next session. Describes the "cost" of not saving. Related to AUG-0028 (Capture Reflex), AUG-0229 (The Moment Bookmark), and AUG-0280 (The Unshared Brilliance).

The lasting change in one's own perspective on a topic triggered by an AI interaction. Describes a sustainable effect: the user sees the topic differently even after the session than before. Related to AUG-0054 (Augmented Understanding), AUG-0149 (The Lasting Impact Question), and AUG-0089 (The Pattern Sharpening).

The habit of capturing particularly successful or noteworthy AI interactions via screenshot — as a personal archive of insights, formulations, or aha moments. Related to AUG-0229 (The Moment Bookmark), AUG-0028 (Capture Reflex), and AUG-0228 (The Version Control Self).

An AI-assisted prepared message that the user ultimately does not send — because the preparation itself already provided the needed clarity or because the user decides otherwise. Related to AUG-0274 (The Message Drafting), AUG-0155 (The Decision Unburdening), and AUG-0019 (Semantic Ejection).

The perspective of individuals who deliberately or out of skepticism begin integrating AI into their work or life process only late. Describes the observation that late adoption carries both disadvantages (missed Adoption Window, AUG-0099) and advantages (learning from others' mistakes, more stable technology). Related to AUG-0099 (The Adoption Window), AUG-0111 (The Augmentation Gap), and AUG-0104 (The Non-Force Principle).

The use of AI for systematic preparation for expected discussions or negotiations — through playing through counterarguments, strength-gentle analysis of one's own position, and developing response strategies. Related to AUG-0289 (The What-If Run), AUG-0040 (Perspective Triangulation), and AUG-0237 (The Invisible Wingman).

The practice of creating a structured summary of what was accomplished at the end of an AI-assisted workday — as accountability to oneself and as a handover document for the next day. Related to AUG-0190 (The Goodnight Integration) and AUG-0140 (The Weekly Status).

The use of AI for formulating diplomatic rejections, apologies, or excuses — when the user has made the substantive decision but lacks the appropriate wording. Related to AUG-0274 (The Message Drafting), AUG-0115 (Social Aerodynamics), and AUG-0026 (The Smooth Shield).

The individual closing sequence with which a user systematically ends their AI sessions — creating summaries, noting open points, saving results, preparing context for the next session. Related to AUG-0073 (The Disconnect Protocol), AUG-0234 (The Soft Landing), and AUG-0190 (The Goodnight Integration).

The conscious reconnection with one's own starting state — the skills, convictions, and working methods before AI use — as a reference point for evaluating one's own developmental path. Related to AUG-0004 (Zero-Point Self), AUG-0228 (The Version Control Self), and AUG-0165 (The Growth Marker).

A user who primarily operates AI via smartphone — typing with their thumb, in short inputs, often on the go. Describes a mobile interaction pattern characterized by brevity, immediacy, and lower input precision. Related to AUG-0137 (Voice-First Protocol), AUG-0276 (The Steady Stream), and Taxonomy Dimension 5 (Interaction Mode).

The use of AI to overcome linguistic, formal, or bureaucratic barriers by individuals whose strengths lie in practical, manual, or operational areas. Describes the observation that AI makes administrative and written work more accessible to people whose core competence is not in writing. Related to AUG-0119 (The Level Playing Field), AUG-0156 (The Articulation Unlock), and AUG-0336 (The Form Slayer).

The moment when a child independently asks an AI system a question for the first time — and the observation of how uninhibited, direct, and non-strategic children communicate with AI. Related to AUG-0162 (The Generational Bridge), Forecast 2 (Education), and AUG-0198 (The New Literacy).

The AI interaction that takes place together with another person — both looking at the same screen and discussing the AI responses. Describes a social form of AI use that breaks the single-user paradigm. Related to AUG-0146 (The Shared Mind), AUG-0265 (The Generation Connector), and AUG-0117 (The Teaching Reflex).

A user whose AI intensity fluctuates depending on life phase, project load, or season — sometimes intensive daily use, sometimes weeks-long pauses. Describes the observation that AI use need not be a linear process. Related to AUG-0141 (The Symbiosis Spectrum), AUG-0120 (The Range Framework), and AUG-0316 (The Own Pace).

The observation that the quality of AI use strongly depends on the user's educational background, language competence, and technical equipment — those who formulate better inputs achieve better results. Related to AUG-0111 (The Augmentation Gap), AUG-0097 (The Competence Premium), and AUG-0106 (The Inclusivity Imperative).

The use of AI on behalf of other people — such as when an AI-competent user conducts research, formulations, or problem-solving for family members, friends, or colleagues. Related to AUG-0265 (The Generation Connector), AUG-0117 (The Teaching Reflex), and AUG-0095 (The One-Person Operation).

The conscious decision to use AI only for basic, uncomplicated tasks — looking up facts, formulating simple texts, quick translations — without using advanced capabilities. Related to AUG-0141 (The Symbiosis Spectrum), AUG-0316 (The Own Pace), and AUG-0147 (The Slow Integration Principle).

The playful, humorous, or culturally coded use of AI — such as generating comparisons, analogies, or formulations that function as "memes" in everyday language. Describes the integration of AI into informal communication culture. Related to AUG-0110 (The Joy Imperative) and AUG-0260 (The Plot Twist Partner).

The observation that older users, despite less technical familiarity, often conduct deeper and more context-rich AI interactions — because they bring more life experience, domain knowledge, and critical distance. Related to AUG-0162 (The Generational Bridge), AUG-0098 (Thinking Leverage), and AUG-0097 (The Competence Premium).

The quiet, externally uncommunicated decision of a previously skeptical user to integrate AI into their work process — without public announcement or discussion. Related to AUG-0100 (The Quiet Competence), AUG-0295 (The Late Adopter View), and AUG-0121 (The Threshold Moment).

The moment when a user pauses while reading a long AI output — because a specific sentence, formulation, or thought demands particular attention. Describes the microdynamics of reading AI texts. Related to AUG-0031 (Semantic Spark), AUG-0022 (Vigilant Continuity), and AUG-0229 (The Moment Bookmark).

A skill that the user possesses only in combination with AI support — without AI, they cannot reproduce it. Describes a competence bound to the AI that is unavailable when the AI is removed. Related to AUG-0056 (The Craft Fade), AUG-0244 (The Instant Expert), and AUG-0004 (Zero-Point Self).

The cumulative effect of repeated AI-assisted communication where the used tonality does not match the user's actual style — and recipients over time notice a discrepancy between digital and personal communication. Related to AUG-0188 (Tone Alignment), AUG-0259 (The Accent Eraser), and AUG-0272 (The Authorship Suspicion).

An idea generated in an AI session that is neither followed up on nor saved and thereby shifted — orphaned in a terminated session. Related to AUG-0280 (The Unshared Brilliance), AUG-0291 (The Forgetting Tax), and AUG-0028 (Capture Reflex).

The individual pace at which a user develops their AI competence — independent of societal pressure, comparison with others, or perceived expectations. Related to AUG-0147 (The Slow Integration Principle), AUG-0104 (The Non-Force Principle), and AUG-0141 (The Symbiosis Spectrum).

The conscious decision to leave a linguistic error, irregularity, or personal idiosyncrasy in an AI-edited text — to preserve the human handwriting. Related to Axiom 12 (Version Truth), AUG-0179 (The Ownership Check), and AUG-0263 (The Ownership Boost).

The use of AI as support for parenting questions in moments of uncertainty — such as for children's questions about difficult topics, formulating age-appropriate explanations, or searching for pedagogical strategies. Related to AUG-0216 (The Parenting Update), AUG-0254 (The Parenting Shortcut), and AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence).

A deliberately challenging or provocative input aimed at drawing the AI out of its standard responses and producing deeper, more original, or more contrarian answers. Related to Axiom 2 (Productive Friction) and AUG-0085 (Latent Space Exploration).

The subtle, not explicitly communicated demonstration of AI competence — such as through the quality of results, the speed of delivery, or the breadth of covered knowledge, without mentioning the AI use. Related to AUG-0100 (The Quiet Competence), AUG-0153 (The Quiet Authority), and AUG-0286 (The Applause Gap).

The tendency to continue an unproductive AI session because much time and context has already been invested — even though a fresh start would be more efficient. Describes a form of sunk-cost thinking in AI interaction. Related to AUG-0159 (The Fresh Start), AUG-0069 (The Optimization Loop), and AUG-0030 (Contextual Gravity).

The gradual improvement of one's own AI use that happens without conscious effort — through pure habituation, repetition, and accumulation of experience. Related to AUG-0195 (The Invisible Growth), AUG-0088 (Algorithmic Intuition), and AUG-0218 (The Independent Upgrade).

The gradual blending of one's own vocabulary with that of the AI — the user unconsciously adopts technical terms, formulation patterns, or expressions encountered through AI interaction. Related to AUG-0283 (The Syntax Voice), AUG-0204 (The Conversational Afterimage), and AUG-0262 (The Echo Sibling).

An unexpected AI-generated idea or perspective that emerges in the final minutes of a session — often just when the user intended to end the session. Related to AUG-0031 (Semantic Spark), AUG-0070 (The Surprise Field), and AUG-0227 (The Late Idea).

The final text or summary a user creates at the end of a longer AI collaboration — as closure for a project, a phase, or a working relationship with a specific system. Related to AUG-0151 (The Release Exhale), AUG-0299 (The Closing Routine), and AUG-0150 (The Unfinished Symphony).

The pride that arises when a user assembles individual parts from different AI interactions into a coherent whole — the achievement lies not in creating the parts but in their assembly. Related to AUG-0081 (Post-Authorial Pride), AUG-0082 (The Curator's Dilemma), and AUG-0263 (The Ownership Boost).

The tendency to disclose more personal information in late-night AI sessions than the user would share with clearer awareness. Related to AUG-0154 (The Late-Night Honesty Window), AUG-0222 (The Oversharing Drift), and Axiom 16 (Data Awareness).

The step-by-step increase in linguistic complexity that a user achieves in a foreign language through repeated AI interaction — each session builds on the previous one. Related to AUG-0169 (The Second-Language Fluency), AUG-0267 (The Language Unlock), and AUG-0195 (The Invisible Growth).

The practice of consciously accepting an AI draft despite known imperfections and continuing to work with it — knowing that perfection would block the work process. Related to AUG-0108 (The Imperfection Clause), AUG-0243 (The Ugly Draft), and Axiom 14 (The First Draft Principle).

The user's uncertainty about whether a specific thought, formulation, or idea originates from themselves or from the AI — a consequence of the Blending Effect (AUG-0007). Related to AUG-0007 (The Blending Effect), AUG-0061 (The Creator's Question), and Axiom 12 (Version Truth).

A neologism describing the totality of all people who actively and consciously live and work in AI collaboration — as a descriptive category, not a value judgment. Related to the Augmanitai concept (AUG-0001), AUG-0141 (The Symbiosis Spectrum), and AUG-0148 (The Augmanitai Manifesto).

The strategy of beginning a new AI project with a deliberately blank page — without a prefabricated template, without prior context, to obtain maximum creative freedom. Related to AUG-0193 (The Open Field), AUG-0159 (The Fresh Start), and AUG-0059 (The Blank Cursor).

The relieving experience when AI helps navigate bureaucratic forms, applications, or administrative texts — tasks that many people find particularly unpleasant and intimidating. Related to AUG-0336 (The Form Slayer), AUG-0302 (The Blue Collar Bypass), and AUG-0236 (The Relief Sigh).

→ Reference to AUG-0162 (The Generational Bridge). In a narrower sense, describes the concrete act of knowledge mediation between generations in the context of AI use — the "bridge-building" as an active endeavor. Related to AUG-0162 (The Generational Bridge) and AUG-0265 (The Generation Connector).

The point in an AI session where the number of parallel thought strands, open questions, and unstructured ideas reaches a level that demands ordering — named after a plate of spaghetti where everything is entangled. Related to AUG-0017 (The Concept Cloud), AUG-0033 (Ebulliometric Sorting), and AUG-0065 (The Information Flood).

The specific use of AI for filling out, understanding, or formulating formal documents — tax returns, insurance applications, job applications, government forms. Related to AUG-0333 (The Bureaucracy Hug), AUG-0302 (The Blue Collar Bypass), and AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence).

The surgical revision of texts with AI support — word by word, sentence by sentence — to find the exact formulation that most precisely expresses the user's thought. Related to AUG-0188 (Tone Alignment), AUG-0136 (The Iteration Discipline), and the Refiner Profile (Profile 2).

The targeted alignment of AI output to a given tone — such as a company's communication style, a specific author's writing style, or the cultural expectations of a recipient. Related to AUG-0188 (Tone Alignment), AUG-0135 (Persona Engineering), and AUG-0026 (The Smooth Shield).

The regular review of whether one's own AI use aligns with one's own values, principles, and boundaries. Describes a reflection practice for the integrity of AI collaboration. Related to AUG-0076 (Self-Referential Grounding), AUG-0024 (The Built-In Compass), and AUG-0140 (The Weekly Status).

The use of AI as a uncertainty-free practice space — for testing formulations, trying out strategies, or simulating conversations before deploying them in the real world. Related to AUG-0289 (The What-If Run), AUG-0296 (The Argument Prep), and AUG-0247 (The Safe Release).

The personal, unshared knowledge a user has about the most effective strategies, wordings, and techniques in interaction with a specific AI system. Describes the implicit knowledge that forms through experience and represents an individual advantage. Related to AUG-0088 (Algorithmic Intuition) and AUG-0097 (The Competence Premium).

A self-reinforcing cycle in which an AI response awakens the user's curiosity, leading to a follow-up question, whose answer in turn generates new curiosity. Describes a productive spiral of knowledge acquisition. Related to AUG-0110 (The Joy Imperative), AUG-0020 (Recursive Feedback Loop), and AUG-0343 (The Thorough Exploration).

An AI session deliberately aimed at maximum depth and breadth of a topic — the user systematically explores all facets, perspectives, and connections. Related to AUG-0342 (The Curiosity Loop), AUG-0085 (Latent Space Exploration), and AUG-0054 (Augmented Understanding).

The use of free or low-cost AI tools by users with limited budgets to overcome access barriers to knowledge, formulation assistance, or consultation. Describes the democratizing potential of freely accessible AI. Related to AUG-0119 (The Level Playing Field), AUG-0306 (The Class Divide Prompt), and AUG-0106 (The Inclusivity Imperative).

The moment a user encounters the limits of an AI system — the AI cannot solve the task, provides faulty information, or reveals comprehension limits. Describes the productive experience of learning a system's limitations. Related to Axiom 9 (Productive Skepticism), AUG-0177 (The Trust Setting), and AUG-0176 (The Capability Discovery).

The use of AI for understanding cultural differences, social norms, or local customs — such as when preparing for international business meetings, travel, or intercultural communication. Related to AUG-0115 (Social Aerodynamics), AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence), and AUG-0237 (The Invisible Wingman).

An interesting piece of information, anecdote, or perspective gained through AI research that the user deploys in conversations — as an icebreaker, conversation contribution, or knowledge demonstration. Related to AUG-0244 (The Instant Expert), AUG-0320 (The Silent Flex), and AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence).

The use of AI as a consultation instance for personal or professional decisions — not as a substitute for professional advice but as initial orientation and structuring tool. Related to AUG-0155 (The Decision Unburdening), AUG-0289 (The What-If Run), and AUG-0208 (The Authority Question).

The technique of asking the AI to consider a question from the perspective of one's own future self — "What would I say about this decision in five years?" Describes a reflection tool for long-term perspective. Related to AUG-0270 (The Future Letter), AUG-0135 (Persona Engineering), and AUG-0114 (The Perspective Range).

The experience of receiving an unvarnished, direct answer from an AI that the user might not have heard from other people — because the AI does not take the social considerations that would prevent an honest statement. Related to Axiom 2 (Productive Friction), AUG-0247 (The Safe Release), and AUG-0319 (The Friction Prompt).

The casual deployment of AI knowledge in everyday conversations — such as contributing historical details, facts, or connections the user previously researched via AI. Related to AUG-0347 (The Party Fact), AUG-0320 (The Silent Flex), and AUG-0244 (The Instant Expert).

The personal collection of AI-assisted results, formulations, and insights that a user accumulates over time — as a kind of knowledge vessel that is reopened when needed. Related to AUG-0229 (The Moment Bookmark), AUG-0293 (The Screenshot Diary), and AUG-0144 (The Open Questions Repository).

The use of AI to discreetly close a knowledge gap in social or professional situations before it becomes visible — such as quickly looking up a term during a meeting. Related to AUG-0237 (The Invisible Wingman), AUG-0244 (The Instant Expert), and AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence).

The targeted use of AI to uncover one's own unspoken assumptions in an argument, plan, or decision. Describes a self-examination practice in which the AI serves as a mirror for thinking premises. Related to AUG-0040 (Perspective Triangulation), Axiom 9 (Productive Skepticism), and AUG-0171 (The Self-Encounter).

The playful use of AI for consultation in everyday aesthetic decisions — clothing, furnishing, color combinations, gift wrapping. Describes a low-threshold everyday application. Related to AUG-0251 (The Kitchen Table), AUG-0257 (The Gift Whisperer), and AUG-0110 (The Joy Imperative).

The use of AI to make everyday routine tasks more interesting, structured, or playful — such as through checklists, time competitions, or creative reformulations of boring tasks. Related to AUG-0110 (The Joy Imperative), AUG-0251 (The Kitchen Table), and AUG-0062 (The Lightness Factor).

The humorous reaction to an obvious AI error — an inappropriate word, an absurd recommendation, or a nonsensical formulation that makes the user laugh. Describes the playful handling of AI imperfection. Related to AUG-0084 (Glitch-Mining), AUG-0083 (The Glitch Wave), and AUG-0110 (The Joy Imperative).

The intense enthusiasm that arises when a user employs the AI in a creative role — as storyteller, game skilled practitioner, or fictional conversation partner — and the quality of the interaction exceeds expectations. Related to AUG-0135 (Persona Engineering), AUG-0260 (The Plot Twist Partner), and AUG-0110 (The Joy Imperative).

The conscious decision to complete a task or time period entirely without AI — as a test, exercise, or personal challenge. Related to AUG-0207 (The Return to Manual), AUG-0055 (Strategic Competence Throttling), and Axiom 20 (Periodic Disconnection).

The observation that AI-assisted collaboration takes different patterns in collectivist cultures than in individualist ones — such as stronger emphasis on group benefit, communal decision-making, and shared access. Related to AUG-0106 (The Inclusivity Imperative) and Band 4 (Cultural Expansion).

The state of an AI session where, after a long phase of intensive interaction, silence suddenly sets in — the user has no more questions, the AI waits, and the space feels "empty." Related to AUG-0126 (Semantic Saturation), AUG-0068 (The Disconnect Signal), and AUG-0180 (The Enough Signal).

The feeling of satisfaction when a user has successfully passed on their AI knowledge to another person and sees that person benefit from it. Related to AUG-0117 (The Teaching Reflex), AUG-0265 (The Generation Connector), and AUG-0113 (Generational Bridge Protocol).

The practice of formulating AI inputs consciously or unconsciously in a mix of two or more languages — and the observation that modern AI systems often handle this mixed form well. Related to AUG-0169 (The Second-Language Fluency), AUG-0267 (The Language Unlock), and AUG-0137 (Voice-First Protocol).

The use of AI as an outlet for thoughts, frustrations, or reflections that the user does not want to or cannot share with other people for social reasons. Related to AUG-0247 (The Safe Release), AUG-0167 (The Digital Confidant Drift), and AUG-0154 (The Late-Night Honesty Window).

The experience of suddenly getting stuck in an everyday AI application — such as when the AI suggests a recipe the user cannot execute, or gives a recommendation that misses the context. Describes tension in everyday applications. Related to AUG-0345 (The Wall Check), AUG-0212 (The Translation Gap), and AUG-0067 (The Glass Wall Effect).

The moment of hesitation before a user copies an AI output and pastes it into another document — the brief internal check: "Is this really as good as I think? Can I take responsibility for it?" Related to Axiom 6 (3-Second Delay), AUG-0179 (The Ownership Check), and AUG-0023 (Vigilance Imperative).

The use of AI for support in formulating personally significant texts — vows, speeches, acknowledgments, eulogies — where both linguistic quality and personal authenticity are critical. Related to AUG-0337 (The Lyric Surgery), AUG-0317 (The Kept Typo), and AUG-0081 (Post-Authorial Pride).

The brief pleasure a user feels when the AI delivers a particularly elegant, apt, or surprisingly beautiful formulation. Related to AUG-0194 (The Positive Surprise), AUG-0110 (The Joy Imperative), and AUG-0031 (Semantic Spark).

The use of AI for researching guidelines, regulations, standards, or best practices in a specific field — as an entry point for further deepening by the user. Related to AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence), AUG-0107 (The Verification Principle), and AUG-0346 (The Culture Decode).

The use of AI for quick information gathering on pet care questions — feeding, behavior, health prevention, species-appropriate husbandry. Describes a specific everyday application of Just-in-Time Competence (AUG-0043). Related to AUG-0251 (The Kitchen Table) and AUG-0216 (The Parenting Update).

The use of AI for quick analysis, statistics queries, or strategy discussion in the sports domain — as a tool for fans, coaches, or sports enthusiasts. Related to AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence), AUG-0347 (The Party Fact), and AUG-0251 (The Kitchen Table).

The use of AI as a communication buffer for individuals who find social interactions taxing — such as through prepared responses, formulated emails, or structured conversation guides. Related to AUG-0115 (Social Aerodynamics), AUG-0237 (The Invisible Wingman), and AUG-0274 (The Message Drafting).

The shortest form of AI interaction — a single question, a single answer, immediately moving on. Describes quick fact-checking or term clarification without an in-depth session. Related to AUG-0308 (The Simple Mode), AUG-0276 (The Steady Stream), and AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence).

The tendency to perceive vague or generally phrased AI responses as personally relevant and accurate — comparable to the Barnum effect with horoscopes. Describes a perception bias in AI interaction. Related to AUG-0064 (The Story Loop), AUG-0039 (Kinetic Truth Blur), and Axiom 9 (Productive Skepticism).

The user's awareness that the AI possesses no genuine intelligence, understanding, or consciousness — but processes statistical patterns that can appear as understanding. Describes a fundamental stance of informed AI use. Related to AUG-0006 (Platform Ontology), Axiom 9 (Productive Skepticism), and AUG-0161 (The Invisible Colleague).

The practice of taking away only a small, targeted knowledge morsel from an AI session — instead of processing the entire response. Describes an efficient approach to information overflow. Related to AUG-0038 (Data Stoicism), AUG-0373 (The Quick Check), and AUG-0065 (The Information Flood).

An AI-triggered creative impulse that motivates the user to start their own project, pursue an idea further, or take a new direction. Related to AUG-0031 (Semantic Spark), AUG-0235 (The Brainstorm Spark), and AUG-0182 (Spark Flight).

The uncertainty of a third party about whether a text, message, or contribution was composed by a human or an AI — named after the Turing Test. Related to AUG-0272 (The Authorship Suspicion), AUG-0314 (The Tone Debt), and Forecast 4 (Culture: Human-Made Premium Label).

The AI's ability to explain complex subject matter in a way that becomes understandable for the user's specific knowledge level — a bridge between technical language and everyday understanding. Related to AUG-0206 (The Understanding Dial), AUG-0067 (The Glass Wall Effect), and Axiom 10 (The Translation Principle).

The experience of an AI-assisted text being flagged as "AI-generated" by a plagiarism or AI detection tool — despite the user having made significant personal contributions. Related to AUG-0272 (The Authorship Suspicion), AUG-0286 (The Applause Gap), and Forecast 6 (Regulation).

The attention with which employers and HR departments observe employees' and applicants' AI use — and the resulting user uncertainty about the acceptance of AI support in the work context. Related to AUG-0272 (The Authorship Suspicion), AUG-0103 (The Openbook Commitment), and Forecast 3 (Organizations).

The moment when an experienced AI user consciously leaves behind the architecture of their AI use and develops a completely new approach — because the existing framework has reached its limits. Related to AUG-0044 (Unlearning Protocol), AUG-0130 (The Integration Frontier), and Phase 5 (Architecture Design).

The point at which the accumulated context volume in an AI session becomes so large that response quality measurably declines — the AI begins to contradict itself, forget information, or drift thematically. Related to AUG-0134 (Context Window Awareness), AUG-0030 (Contextual Gravity), and AUG-0159 (The Fresh Start).

The targeted use of AI to test one's own knowledge — by asking the AI to question one's assumptions, provide counterarguments, or point out weaknesses in one's reasoning. Related to AUG-0354 (The Assumption Hunter), Axiom 2 (Productive Friction), and AUG-0319 (The Friction Prompt).

The experience of "going out on a limb" in an AI-assisted foreign language situation — attempting more complex sentences than one would dare alone. Describes the expanded uncertainty-taking in language production through AI support. Related to AUG-0169 (The Second-Language Fluency), AUG-0328 (The Language Ladder), and AUG-0232 (The Courage Click).

The experience that voice-controlled AI interaction feels qualitatively different from text-based — more personal, more immediate, but also less controllable. Describes the difference between modalities. Related to AUG-0137 (Voice-First Protocol), AUG-0301 (The Thumb Thinker), and Taxonomy Dimension 5 (Interaction Mode).

The feeling of success when using an AI-supported argument in a discussion and convincing with it — and the simultaneous question of whether the credit belongs to the user or the AI. Related to AUG-0296 (The Argument Prep), AUG-0330 (The Origin Doubt), and AUG-0081 (Post-Authorial Pride).

The feeling of "owing something" to an AI because it delivered an idea the user successfully implemented — despite the AI having no consciousness and being unable to receive gratitude. Related to AUG-0128 (The Gratitude Response), AUG-0220 (The Gratitude Paradox), and AUG-0275 (The Parasocial Slip).

The observation that children can surpass their parents in AI competence — and the resulting dynamic in which the traditional knowledge hierarchy between parents and children shifts. Related to AUG-0162 (The Generational Bridge), AUG-0303 (The Child's First Prompt), and Forecast 2 (Education).

The role an AI can assume in nighttime sessions — as a quiet, judgment-free conversation partner for thoughts the user does not express during the day. Related to AUG-0185 (The Late-Night Ally), AUG-0167 (The Digital Confidant Drift), and AUG-0364 (The Silent Outlet).

The systematic practice of checking AI outputs for factual accuracy before further use — through counter-research, source comparison, or expert consultation. Related to AUG-0049 (Cross-Referential Validation), AUG-0107 (The Verification Principle), and Axiom 17 (Source Discipline).

The gradual change in one's own writing style through regular AI use — the user unconsciously adopts structures, lengths, or formulation preferences of the AI system. Related to AUG-0283 (The Syntax Voice), AUG-0323 (The Vocabulary Blur), and AUG-0125 (The Feedback Effect).

The gradual shift of one's own memory performance to AI systems — the user remembers less because they know the information is retrievable at any time. Related to AUG-0045 (Indexical Memory), AUG-0015 (The Outer Mind), and AUG-0056 (The Skill Fade).

A question that the user only learns to ask through AI interaction — the AI response to a simple question reveals a deeper level that enables a more complex follow-up question. Related to AUG-0342 (The Curiosity Loop), AUG-0054 (Augmented Understanding), and AUG-0089 (The Pattern Sharpening).

An AI session or collaboration that extends over weeks or months — with growing context, increasing depth, and a developing shared frame of reference. Related to AUG-0231 (The Warm Start) and AUG-0075 (The Gardener Protocol).

The specific application of the 3-Second Delay (Axiom 6) to AI-generated code — the practice of pausing before executing AI-written code and reviewing it line by line. Related to Axiom 6 (3-Second Delay), AUG-0366 (The Copy Pause), and AUG-0023 (Vigilance Imperative).

The conscious delay between receiving an AI response and the next input — used to process the response before posing the next question. Related to AUG-0197 (The Shared Quiet), AUG-0178 (The Delayed Processing), and Axiom 6 (3-Second Delay).

The use of AI as a tireless instructor for hobbies and leisure activities — from musical instruments to gardening to chess or photography. Related to AUG-0268 (The Homework Stream), AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence), and AUG-0110 (The Joy Imperative).

The use of AI for highly specialized, rare, or unusual questions for which there are few other information sources — the AI as access to niche knowledge. Related to AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence), AUG-0085 (Latent Space Exploration), and AUG-0343 (The Thorough Exploration).

A brief, regular self-check — "How am I using AI right now? Does this feel right? Am I still aligned with my principles?" — established as a daily or weekly rhythm. Related to AUG-0077 (The Status-Update Signal), AUG-0339 (The Principle Check), and AUG-0140 (The Weekly Status).

The systematic use of AI to juxtapose the pros and cons of a decision — as a structuring aid for one's own decision-making process. Related to AUG-0289 (The What-If Run), AUG-0155 (The Decision Unburdening), and AUG-0040 (Perspective Triangulation).

The distortion of one's own perception that arises when the user repeatedly uses AI outputs as a benchmark — causing the AI perspective to unconsciously become the standard against which reality is measured. Related to AUG-0230 (The Algorithmic Filter), AUG-0217 (The Echo Chamber of One), and AUG-0072 (Memetic Firewall).

The relief that arises when AI handles a complex foreign language requirement — such as understanding a legal document, formulating a business email, or communicating with an authority in another language. Related to AUG-0169 (The Second-Language Fluency), AUG-0236 (The Relief Sigh), and AUG-0302 (The Blue Collar Bypass).

The ratio between the effort a user invests in an AI input and the value of the received output — the observation that experienced users achieve higher-value results with less input effort. Related to AUG-0092 (Output Asymmetry), AUG-0097 (The Competence Premium), and AUG-0133 (Prompt Craftsmanship).

The metaphor for an AI system that displays impressive abilities but surprisingly fall short in certain areas — comparable to a gifted child who unexpectedly stumbles in some everyday situations. Describes the asymmetric competence distribution of AI systems. Related to AUG-0345 (The Wall Check), AUG-0006 (Platform Ontology), and AUG-0375 (The Simulation Awareness).

The unsettling experience when an AI response is formulated so accurately, personally, or flatteringly that the user briefly feels the AI is deliberately "courting" them. Related to AUG-0245 (The Seen Feeling), AUG-0288 (The Kindness Shock), and AUG-0374 (The Horoscope Drift).

The use of AI for filtering, summarizing, and contextualizing news content — as a tool against news overload and for gaining a structured overview. Related to AUG-0038 (Data Stoicism), AUG-0065 (The Information Flood), and AUG-0181 (The Top View).

The use of AI to defuse conflict situations before they arise — through better formulations, prepared compromise proposals, or simulation of the opposing perspective. Related to AUG-0052 (Conflict Resolution by Proxy), AUG-0296 (The Argument Prep), and AUG-0115 (Social Aerodynamics).

The use of AI in the evening hours for analysis, discussion, or contextualization of films, series, books, or other cultural content — as an intellectual conversation partner after consumption. Related to AUG-0249 (The Lullaby Loop), AUG-0342 (The Curiosity Loop), and AUG-0110 (The Joy Imperative).

The use of AI for reconstructing or structuring personal memories — such as through chronological ordering of life events, summarizing past projects, or contextualizing old notes. Related to AUG-0045 (Indexical Memory), AUG-0352 (The Memory Jar), and AUG-0228 (The Version Control Self).

The use of AI to close knowledge gaps quickly and specifically — without the aspiration for deep understanding, but as pragmatic bridging for the moment. Related to AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence), AUG-0373 (The Quick Check), and AUG-0353 (The Face Saver).

The temptation to use AI outputs directly as a decision basis without sufficient review — facilitated by time pressure, convenience, or excessive trust. Related to AUG-0422 (The Unchecked Trust), AUG-0219 (The Decision Handoff), and Axiom 1 (Asymmetric Responsibility).

The tendency to keep scrolling through AI outputs, asking follow-up questions, and touching on new topics without setting a completion point — a digital equivalent of aimless scrolling in social media. Related to AUG-0069 (The Optimization Loop), AUG-0068 (The Disconnect Signal), and AUG-0180 (The Enough Signal).

A deliberately skeptical or cynical input aimed at moving the AI toward a more sober, less optimistic response — as a counterweight to the tendency toward positive default tonality in AI systems. Related to AUG-0319 (The Friction Prompt), Axiom 2 (Productive Friction), and AUG-0384 (The Knowledge Challenger).

The role of AI as a constantly available, background-running advisor — the user does not actively access the AI but knows it is immediately available if needed, and this knowledge alone already changes their behavior. Related to AUG-0143 (Ambient Thinking Support), AUG-0253 (The Quiet Co-Pilot), and AUG-0015 (The Outer Mind).

The uncertainty that AI-assisted communication enables a flawless external presentation that does not correspond to the user's actual competence or actual state. Related to AUG-0314 (The Tone Debt), AUG-0244 (The Instant Expert), and AUG-0272 (The Authorship Suspicion).

The quick, exploratory use of AI to mentally play through the possible consequences of an action before actually executing it. Related to AUG-0289 (The What-If Run), AUG-0090 (Predictive Vision), and AUG-0340 (The Practice Room).

The use of AI to break entrenched work routines — through new perspectives, alternative methods, or unexpected suggestions that lead the user out of their habitual pattern. Related to AUG-0225 (The Unexpected Voice), AUG-0248 (The Surprise Angle), and AUG-0129 (The Trailblazer Mode).

The use of AI as a silent editor who improves texts without third parties noticing the editing. Related to AUG-0237 (The Invisible Wingman), AUG-0337 (The Lyric Surgery), and AUG-0026 (The Smooth Shield).

The use of AI as a productive alternative to unproductive screen time — instead of aimless scrolling on social media, a brief AI conversation about an interesting topic. Related to AUG-0342 (The Curiosity Loop), AUG-0110 (The Joy Imperative), and AUG-0376 (The Knowledge Sip).

In the Augmanitai context: The practice of systematically feeding back AI-generated results to the AI to improve the quality of subsequent outputs — a conscious cycle of input, evaluation, and refinement. Related to AUG-0020 (Recursive Feedback Loop), AUG-0136 (The Iteration Discipline), and AUG-0133 (Prompt Craftsmanship).

The phenomenon that a user adopts AI outputs without verification — whether from time pressure, convenience, or excessive trust in the system's technical competence. Related to AUG-0412 (The Decision Shortcut), Axiom 1 (Asymmetric Responsibility), and AUG-0177 (The Trust Setting).

The last version of an AI-assisted work that the user declares "finished" — the moment when the Optimization Loop (AUG-0069) is shifted and the Enough Signal (AUG-0180) is acknowledged. Related to AUG-0151 (The Release Exhale), AUG-0180 (The Enough Signal), and Axiom 14 (The First Draft Principle).

The use of AI to research one's own family history, origin, or cultural roots — as an entry point for genealogical research or cultural self-positioning. Related to AUG-0410 (The Memory Lane), AUG-0349 (The Future Self Prompt), and AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence).

The quiet hunch of a user that an AI response is not quite correct — even before they can identify the error. Describes the developed intuition for AI inaccuracies. Related to AUG-0088 (Algorithmic Intuition), AUG-0039 (Kinetic Truth Blur), and AUG-0022 (Vigilant Continuity).

The use of AI for solving very concrete, crafting, or practical problems — such as correcting a knitting pattern, finding repair instructions, or adjusting a recipe. Describes the everyday problem-solving function. Related to AUG-0251 (The Kitchen Table), AUG-0266 (The Recipe Riff), and AUG-0398 (The Hobby Teacher).

The user's expectation that AI systems will be better, faster, and more versatile in the future — and the resulting decision to postpone certain tasks until the technology has matured further. Related to AUG-0224 (The Waiting Game), AUG-0036 (Transient Validity), and AUG-0142 (The Post-Interface Hypothesis).

The feeling of success when AI solves a technical problem the user could not have managed alone — named after the typical example of regular expressions, which are incomprehensible to many users but generated by AI in seconds. Related to AUG-0205 (The Skill Unlock), AUG-0313 (The Ghost Skill), and AUG-0157 (The Competence Rush).

The collection of tested, proven input formulations that a user builds up over time and reuses as needed — a personal prompt archive. Related to AUG-0341 (The Secret Map), AUG-0133 (Prompt Craftsmanship), and AUG-0352 (The Memory Jar).

The return to an old, proven AI workflow after a new approach did not bring the desired success — the recognition that the established sometimes works better than the newest. Related to AUG-0382 (The Architect's Exit), AUG-0277 (The Loyalty Glitch), and AUG-0207 (The Return to Manual).

The internal perception that the moment for an AI interaction has not yet come — the user senses that they must think further on their own first before the AI can add value. Related to AUG-0178 (The Delayed Processing), AUG-0197 (The Shared Quiet), and AUG-0059 (The Blank Cursor).

A particularly striking AI formulation that stays in the user's memory and influences their thinking or speaking long-term. Related to AUG-0204 (The Conversational Afterimage), AUG-0292 (The View Shift), and AUG-0046 (The Felt Echo).

The conscious or forced transition of all session context — and the necessity to rebuild the context from scratch. Related to AUG-0383 (The Context dissolution), AUG-0159 (The Fresh Start), and AUG-0291 (The Forgetting Tax).

The use of AI to find a word or term that is "on the tip of the tongue" — the AI as vocabulary aid for one's own mental lexicon. Related to AUG-0156 (The Articulation Unlock), AUG-0196 (The Words-Before-Words), and AUG-0373 (The Quick Check).

The quick AI query for everyday food decisions — "What can I cook with these ingredients?", "What fits as a side dish?" Describes the lowest-threshold form of AI use in the household. Related to AUG-0266 (The Recipe Riff), AUG-0251 (The Kitchen Table), and AUG-0373 (The Quick Check).

The use of AI to understand jargon or to employ it correctly oneself — as protection against exclusion from technically dominated contexts. Related to AUG-0379 (The Understanding Bridge), AUG-0302 (The Blue Collar Bypass), and AUG-0206 (The Understanding Dial).

The AI-assisted optimization of the daily schedule — sorting appointments, planning buffer times, reordering priorities. Describes the use of AI as a tool for personal time organization. Related to AUG-0158 (The Morning Setup), AUG-0138 (The Session Architecture), and AUG-0096 (Attention-to-Value Conversion).

The state of accelerated thinking triggered by a particularly productive AI session — the user thinks faster, connects more, and has the feeling of running at full intellectual capacity. Related to AUG-0221 (The Thinking Boost), AUG-0152 (The Focus Surge), and AUG-0122 (Symbiotic Work State).

The use of AI to mentally anticipate an upcoming situation — how will the meeting go, what questions might arise, how will the presentation be received. Related to AUG-0289 (The What-If Run), AUG-0296 (The Argument Prep), and AUG-0340 (The Practice Room).

The observation that some users feel "incomplete" or limited without AI access — as if a part of their thinking capacity were unavailable. Related to AUG-0015 (The Outer Mind), AUG-0393 (The Memory Outsourcing), and AUG-0056 (The Skill Fade).

The conscious decision not to use certain AI outputs — not because they are incorrect but because they do not fit the personal style, one's own values, or the intended expression. Related to AUG-0019 (Semantic Ejection), AUG-0024 (The Built-In Compass), and AUG-0076 (Self-Referential Grounding).

The experience that cloud-based AI systems have no memory of past sessions and the user must start from scratch each time — and the tension that results. Related to AUG-0291 (The Forgetting Tax), AUG-0433 (The Context Wipe), and AUG-0231 (The Warm Start).

The notion that the AI-assisted version of one's own output — texts, presentations, communication — represents a "better self" that one would not achieve without AI. Related to AUG-0416 (The Perfect Front), AUG-0166 (The Borrowed Confidence), and AUG-0004 (Zero-Point Self).

The use of AI for preparation of negotiations, complaints, or legal disputes in everyday life — such as rental disputes, complaints, or insurance cases. Related to AUG-0296 (The Argument Prep), AUG-0336 (The Form Slayer), and AUG-0302 (The Blue Collar Bypass).

The use of AI for contextualizing notable information — such as placing a news item in context, evaluating a uncertainty, or relativizing a concern through fact-checking. Related to AUG-0407 (The News Filter), AUG-0038 (Data Stoicism), and AUG-0348 (The Digital Counsel).

The practice of having AI create an outline or structure that the user then fills with content themselves — the AI delivers the framework, the human the content. Related to AUG-0243 (The Ugly Draft), Axiom 14 (The First Draft Principle), and AUG-0138 (The Session Architecture).

The perception of the AI as a system that mirrors one's own thoughts back — sometimes in improved, sometimes in distorted form. Describes the mirror function of AI. Related to AUG-0170 (The Witness Effect), AUG-0171 (The Self-Encounter), and AUG-0217 (The Echo Chamber of One).

The consciously superficial AI query that does not aim for depth — quick, pragmatic, without claim to completeness. Related to AUG-0373 (The Quick Check), AUG-0376 (The Knowledge Sip), and AUG-0308 (The Simple Mode).

An individual, not publicly documented AI usage journey — the user develops their competence quietly, without blogs, posts, or public statements, sharing their experiences only within the closest circle. Related to AUG-0100 (The Quiet Competence), AUG-0311 (The Quiet Yes), and AUG-0316 (The Own Pace).

The awareness of creative professionals regarding the impact of AI on their field — the question of how authorship, originality, and the value of human creativity change in a world of AI-generated content. Related to Forecast 4 (Culture: Human-Made Premium Label), AUG-0061 (The Creator's Question), and Axiom 18 (Authorship).

A user's foundational understanding of the technical workings of AI systems — particularly the concept of token processing, context windows, and probabilistic text generation. Related to AUG-0134 (Context Window Awareness), AUG-0375 (The Simulation Awareness), and AUG-0006 (Platform Ontology).

The increasing difficulty of distinguishing between AI-generated and human-created content — both for the user themselves and for third parties. Related to AUG-0039 (Kinetic Truth Blur), AUG-0378 (The Turing Suspicion), and Forecast 4 (Culture).

→ Extension of AUG-0166 (The Borrowed Confidence). In a narrower sense, describes the act of short-term "borrowing" of confidence through AI support for a specific situation — such as a presentation, a job interview, or a difficult conversation. Related to AUG-0166 (The Borrowed Confidence) and AUG-0232 (The Courage Click).

A user's conscious knowledge of which of their abilities are AI-supported and which are independent — the ability to distinguish between Ghost Crafts (AUG-0313) and genuine competencies. Related to AUG-0004 (Zero-Point Self), AUG-0313 (The Ghost Craft), and AUG-0165 (The Growth Marker).

The observation that users speak more clearly, structuredly, and consciously during voice-controlled AI interaction than in normal conversations — an adaptation to the technical requirements of speech recognition. Related to AUG-0137 (Voice-First Protocol), AUG-0386 (The Voice Valley), and AUG-0125 (The Feedback Effect).

The visual representation of AI processing time — the blinking dots or loading indicator — and the microdynamics this waiting time triggers in the user: expectation, impatience, intensity, or reflection. Related to AUG-0261 (The Loading Screen Wait), AUG-0048 (Chronometric Gap), and AUG-0197 (The Shared Quiet).

The practice of having another person proofread, verify, or comment on an AI output — as additional quality assurance beyond one's own review. Related to AUG-0160 (The Accountability Anchor), AUG-0079 (The People Standard), and AUG-0050 (The Reality Check).

The systematic, deep-drilling use of AI to explore a topic from the surface to its depths — question by question, layer by layer. Related to AUG-0342 (The Curiosity Loop), AUG-0343 (The Thorough Exploration), and AUG-0394 (The Synthetic Question).

The user's awareness that AI summaries inevitably simplify, filter, and weight — and that every summary represents an interpretation, not a neutral reproduction. Related to AUG-0071 (Epistemic Hygiene), Axiom 9 (Productive Skepticism), and AUG-0402 (The Filter Distortion).

The use of AI for planning outdoor activities — hiking routes, camping equipment, weather assessment, travel planning — as a specific everyday application. Related to AUG-0472 (The Vacation Planner), AUG-0251 (The Kitchen Table), and AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence).

The use of AI to improve communication with a partner or close contacts — such as through formulation assistance for difficult conversations or analysis of misunderstandings. Related to AUG-0115 (Social Aerodynamics), AUG-0408 (The Conflict Avoidance), and AUG-0274 (The Message Drafting).

The quick AI query of a single, specific detail — a date, a number, a name, a formula — without further context need. Related to AUG-0373 (The Quick Check), AUG-0448 (The Surface Lookup), and AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence).

The targeted extraction of the most valuable piece of information from a long AI output — the ability to identify the core of a response and ignore the rest. Related to AUG-0376 (The Knowledge Sip), AUG-0038 (Data Stoicism), and AUG-0312 (The Scroll Pause).

The use of AI for evaluating one's own writing style — readability, clarity, tonality, persuasiveness — as a tool for self-improvement. Related to AUG-0337 (The Lyric Surgery), AUG-0188 (Tone Alignment), and AUG-0171 (The Self-Encounter).

The use of AI as a first sieve for one's own ideas — the user presents an idea to the AI and uses its reaction as an aspect of viability. Related to AUG-0235 (The Brainstorm Spark), AUG-0082 (The Curator's Dilemma), and AUG-0354 (The Assumption Hunter).

The gap in one's own knowledge made visible through AI interaction — the AI reveals what the user does not know by referencing prerequisites, connections, or technical terms unknown to the user. Related to AUG-0171 (The Self-Encounter), AUG-0067 (The Glass Wall Effect), and AUG-0411 (The Gap Filler).

A particularly memorable AI result — a formulation, a comparison, an image — that serves the user as a memory aid and makes a complex subject permanently retrievable. Related to AUG-0432 (The Lasting Voice), AUG-0031 (Semantic Spark), and AUG-0045 (Indexical Memory).

A colloquial, non-scientific designation for the AI system as an everyday companion — without the implication of genuine friendship. Related to AUG-0161 (The Invisible Colleague), AUG-0447 (The Echo Friend), and AUG-0275 (The Parasocial Slip).

The relief that arises when AI helps with creating, analyzing, or debugging spreadsheets — a task many users find particularly tension. Related to AUG-0428 (The Regex Rush), AUG-0236 (The Relief Sigh), and AUG-0205 (The Skill Unlock).

The use of AI to search for the right word, name, title, or term — when the user has an approximate description but cannot find the exact expression. Related to AUG-0434 (The Word Rescue), AUG-0462 (The Detail Lookup), and AUG-0373 (The Quick Check).

→ Synonym/extension of AUG-0206 (The Understanding Dial), applied to tonality rather than complexity. Describes the user's ability to deliberately steer the tonality of AI responses — from formal to informal, from factual to creative. Related to AUG-0206 (The Understanding Dial) and AUG-0188 (Tone Alignment).

The use of AI for comprehensive vacation planning — destinations, routes, accommodations, activities, budget, cultural specifics. Describes one of the most popular everyday AI applications. Related to AUG-0460 (The Outdoor Plan), AUG-0346 (The Culture Decode), and AUG-0251 (The Kitchen Table).

The use of AI for summarizing, analyzing, or contextualizing books — as preparation for one's own reading, as follow-up, or as alternative to books the user cannot read in full. Related to AUG-0459 (The Summary Awareness), AUG-0181 (The Top View), and AUG-0376 (The Knowledge Sip).

The use of AI for quick solving of mathematical problems — from simple calculations through statistical analyses to formula rearrangements. Related to AUG-0428 (The Regex Rush), AUG-0469 (The Spreadsheet Relief), and AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence).

The use of AI for solving everyday technical problems — printer issues, software settings, device configurations — that would overwhelm the user without AI. Related to AUG-0426 (The Knitting Fix), AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence), and AUG-0251 (The Kitchen Table).

→ Extension of AUG-0367 (The Wedding Vow). Describes the specific use of AI for preparing public speeches at personally significant occasions — weddings, birthdays, anniversaries, farewells. Related to AUG-0367 (The Wedding Vow), AUG-0337 (The Lyric Surgery), and AUG-0166 (The Borrowed Confidence).

The targeted use of AI to search for alternative formulations, synonyms, or paraphrases — as a tool for linguistic variety and precision. Related to AUG-0434 (The Word Rescue), AUG-0337 (The Lyric Surgery), and AUG-0133 (Prompt Craftsmanship).

The experience of sudden AI unavailability — through connection transition, server problems, or missing internet access — and the resulting confrontation with one's own AI reliance. Related to AUG-0440 (The Tethered Mind), AUG-0207 (The Return to Manual), and Axiom 15 (The Off-Switch).

→ Extension of AUG-0366 (The Copy Pause), specifically applied to messages. The moment of hesitation before an AI-prepared text is actually sent to the recipient — the final check of whether the message truly corresponds to what the user wants to say. Related to AUG-0366 (The Copy Pause), AUG-0274 (The Message Drafting), and AUG-0294 (The Unsent Draft).

The use of AI to drastically accelerate the learning process for new software, a new tool, or a new skill — through targeted questions instead of linearly working through manuals. Related to AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence), AUG-0205 (The Skill Unlock), and AUG-0398 (The Hobby Teacher).

The confidence gained through AI support to tackle practical projects independently — repairs, renovations, handicrafts — that the user would not have attempted without AI guidance. Related to AUG-0166 (The Borrowed Confidence), AUG-0426 (The Knitting Fix), and AUG-0205 (The Skill Unlock).

The use of AI for identifying further sources, tools, contacts, or institutions — the AI as a guide to human expertise and non-digital resources. Related to AUG-0369 (The Guideline Search), AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence), and Axiom 17 (Source Discipline).

The playful use of AI for creative solving of everyday constraints — such as cooking with leftover ingredients, crafting gifts from available materials, or creating an evening plan with a limited budget. Related to AUG-0266 (The Recipe Riff), AUG-0435 (The Dinner Shortcut), and AUG-0356 (The Chore Gamify).

The practice of giving the AI system a personal name or nickname — as a form of everyday integration that makes the interaction more informal and accessible. Related to AUG-0275 (The Parasocial Slip), AUG-0468 (The Silicon Friend), and AUG-0161 (The Invisible Colleague).

The use of AI for identifying songs, poems, or text passages based on fragmentary memories — "It went something like…" Related to AUG-0470 (The Name Detective), AUG-0434 (The Word Rescue), and AUG-0373 (The Quick Check).

The use of AI as a shield against the burden of email correspondence — through pre-formulating responses, summarizing long email chains, or prioritizing incoming messages. Related to AUG-0183 (The Productivity Shield), AUG-0274 (The Message Drafting), and AUG-0096 (Attention-to-Value Conversion).

The observable gentle of many AI systems in the area of humor — jokes that do not work, irony that is not recognized, or humor that seems inappropriate. Describes a specific AI limitation. Related to AUG-0345 (The Wall Check), AUG-0405 (The Gifted Child), and AUG-0357 (The Glitch Giggle).

A single fact researched through AI that the user deliberately introduces into a discussion to strengthen their position. Related to AUG-0296 (The Argument Prep), AUG-0347 (The Party Fact), and AUG-0387 (The Debate Win).

The use of AI as a launch pad for projects — the AI delivers the initial structure, the first ideas, and the framework on which the user builds. Related to AUG-0446 (The Outline Script), AUG-0377 (The Inspiration Spark), and Axiom 14 (The First Draft Principle).

The use of AI to begin a new hobby — gathering information, researching basic equipment, planning first steps. Related to AUG-0398 (The Hobby Teacher), AUG-0480 (The Tutorial Speedrun), and AUG-0176 (The Capability Discovery).

The practice of naming one's own current state in the AI input — "I'm pressure right now," "I have little time," "I need something simple" — as context provision for the AI. Related to AUG-0133 (Prompt Craftsmanship), AUG-0135 (Persona Engineering), and AUG-0021 (Initialization Cascade).

A brief, regular contact with the AI to review, update, or recalibrate the progress of an ongoing project — comparable to a short status meeting. Related to AUG-0400 (The Status Pulse), AUG-0276 (The Steady Stream), and AUG-0140 (The Weekly Status).

The unnoticed integration of AI use into everyday life — the user barely notices when they use AI because the transitions have become fluid. Related to AUG-0322 (The Quiet Upgrade), AUG-0142 (The Post-Interface Hypothesis), and AUG-0276 (The Steady Stream).

The use of AI as a "rubber duck debugger" — the practice of describing a problem aloud (or in writing) and finding the solution through the act of verbalization itself, even before the AI responds. Named after the debugging concept from software development. Related to AUG-0170 (The Witness Effect), AUG-0156 (The Articulation Unlock), and AUG-0196 (The Words-Before-Words).

The willingness to try new, not yet mature AI features — knowing they may be faulty, but with the curiosity to explore the possibilities. Related to AUG-0129 (The Trailblazer Mode), AUG-0085 (Latent Space Exploration), and AUG-0176 (The Capability Discovery).

The use of AI knowledge as a social buffer — the user can participate in conversations despite not knowing the topic deeply, because they conducted a quick AI research beforehand. Related to AUG-0351 (The Trivia Flex), AUG-0353 (The Face Saver), and AUG-0244 (The Instant Expert).

The use of AI as an external scaffolding for one's own memory — not as a substitute but as a supplement that helps organize, connect, and keep information retrievable. Related to AUG-0045 (Indexical Memory), AUG-0393 (The Memory Outsourcing), and AUG-0467 (The Memory Anchor).

The use of AI to free one's own texts from unnecessary jargon — simplification without substance transition, so the text becomes understandable for a broader audience. Related to AUG-0436 (The Jargon Shield), AUG-0206 (The Understanding Dial), and AUG-0379 (The Understanding Bridge).

The metaphorical function of AI as a "restart button" for stalled projects, thoughts, or situations — a new perspective that frees the user from a dead end. Related to AUG-0159 (The Fresh Start), AUG-0418 (The Routine Breaker), and AUG-0248 (The Surprise Angle).

The use of AI as an invisible travel companion — for real-time translations, orientation, cultural contextualization, and local recommendations during a trip. Related to AUG-0472 (The Vacation Planner), AUG-0346 (The Culture Decode), and AUG-0237 (The Invisible Wingman).

The ability of an experienced user to flexibly adapt their own input style to different AI tasks — formal for business texts, playful for creative projects, technical for programming. Related to AUG-0133 (Prompt Craftsmanship), AUG-0471 (The Tone Dial), and AUG-0088 (Algorithmic Intuition).
